patient_information.address: 4827 Willow Crest Drive, Apartment 3B, Sacramento, CA 95821

treatment_category_and_urgency.diagnosis: Lumbar degenerative disc disease with L4-L5 disc herniation, chronic radiculopathy, and associated chronic pain syndrome

disputed_service_details.description_of_disputed_medical_service_:
The disputed service is a physician-prescribed course of outpatient physical therapy combined with a series of lumbar epidural steroid injections and a short-term trial of a non-formulary neuropathic pain medication (brand name: Neurolexa XR, generic equivalent not tolerated) for treatment of chronic lumbar degenerative disc disease with an L4-L5 herniated disc and nerve root compression. My treating pain management specialist, Dr. Andrea Kim, requested authorization for:

1. Twelve (12) additional sessions of targeted physical therapy over a 6-week period, focusing on core strengthening, lumbar stabilization, and nerve gliding exercises, to be provided by River Park Physical Therapy in Sacramento. I have already completed an initial 8-session course, during which my pain levels went from a 7–8/10 down to a more manageable 4–5/10 on most days, and I was able to increase my walking tolerance from about 5 minutes to 20–25 minutes. The additional sessions were requested specifically to consolidate these gains and avoid escalation to surgical intervention.

2. A series of up to three (3) lumbar epidural steroid injections at the L4-L5 level over a 3-month period. I previously received one epidural injection in February 2024, which provided about 60–70% pain relief for approximately 6 weeks, allowing me to return to part-time work as a warehouse supervisor. My physician’s plan was to repeat the injection at appropriate intervals, based on my response, to keep my pain at a level that allows me to function and avoid long-term opioid use.

3. Coverage for a 90-day trial of Neurolexa XR (a long-acting neuropathic pain medication that is not on the insurance company’s standard formulary) after I failed or could not tolerate three other formulary medications. Specifically, I developed severe gastrointestinal side effects on gabapentin, experienced disabling drowsiness and cognitive fog on pregabalin, and had a documented allergic reaction (rash and facial swelling, treated in urgent care) to duloxetine. Dr. Kim prescribed Neurolexa XR because it has a different side-effect profile and, in her clinical judgment, is the most appropriate next option given my history.

Pacific Horizon Health Plan denied coverage for all three components of this treatment plan, stating that the requested services are “not medically necessary beyond the initial conservative treatment period” and that the non-formulary medication is “not indicated after failure of first-line agents according to the plan’s internal criteria.” I am requesting an Independent Medical Review because my treating physicians strongly disagree with this determination and believe that the denial places me at risk of worsening pain, functional decline, and possible need for more invasive surgery.

I want the Independent Medical Review to consider the full context: I have been dealing with this back condition since a work-related lifting injury in late 2022. I have tried home exercise programs, over-the-counter medications, prescription NSAIDs, muscle relaxants, and an initial limited course of physical therapy. The combination of physical therapy, epidural injections, and the proposed neuropathic medication is intended to allow me to continue working, caring for my 10-year-old daughter, and avoiding long-term opioid dependence or early spinal surgery. The disputed services are therefore central to maintaining my quality of life and ability to remain employed.
disputed_service_details.list_of_physicians_who_have_treated_you_:
1. Andrea Kim, MD – Pain Management Specialist, Sacramento Spine & Pain Center, 2100 Midtown Plaza, Suite 450, Sacramento, CA 95816, phone: (916) 555-3021. Dr. Kim has been my primary pain specialist since January 2024 and is the physician who requested the disputed treatments.

2. Robert L. Hernandez, MD – Primary Care Physician, Midtown Family Health Clinic, 1630 J Street, Sacramento, CA 95814, phone: (916) 555-1142. Dr. Hernandez has been my primary doctor for over 7 years and first evaluated my back injury in late 2022. He coordinated my initial imaging, referrals to physical therapy, and referral to Dr. Kim.

3. Priya Desai, DPT – Physical Therapist, River Park Physical Therapy, 3921 Elmwood Avenue, Suite 210, Sacramento, CA 95821, phone: (916) 555-7360. Dr. Desai has been providing my physical therapy since February 2024 and documented meaningful functional improvement during the initial 8-session course, which is why she and Dr. Kim jointly recommended extending therapy.

4. Jason Wu, MD – Orthopedic Spine Surgeon, Capital Orthopedic Group, 5010 Fair Oaks Boulevard, Suite 120, Sacramento, CA 95864, phone: (916) 555-8893. Dr. Wu evaluated me in April 2024. He confirmed the MRI findings of L4-L5 disc herniation with nerve root compression and recommended that we maximize non-surgical management (including the epidural injections and extended physical therapy) before considering surgery, due to my age and the potential long-term impact of spinal fusion.
